title: Implement Postgres wire-protocol listener for twinning
Resolve twinning v1 boundaries after drift pass
description:
Context:
The first drift pass aligned twinning with PLAN_FACTORY sequencing, removed compare-as-tool assumptions, and updated verify references to the compiled constraint protocol. A second pass is needed to settle the remaining v1 boundaries before deeper implementation work.

Decision topics:
- whether the CLI should keep --rules as the user-facing flag or rename to --constraints / --verify
- whether twinning should execute verify via an embedded library surface or via export-and-run batch invocation in v1
- what belongs in the twinning report versus verify.report / benchmark / assess outputs
- whether Twin A / Twin B migration proof remains in the v1 plan or moves to a later mode after Postgres tournament mode
- whether non-SQL adapters stay in this plan or move to a separate future document
- whether the SQL subset table should be replaced by a canary-defined manifest only

title: Implement bounded-memory overlay and pluggable replay backend for twinning
Build in-memory row store and constraint executor for twinning
Build Postgres compatibility and differential test harness for twinning
Integrate compiled verify constraints and twin metrics reporting
description:
Context:
The twinning drift pass narrowed the contract: twinning does not own a second rules language and should not behave like a standalone scorer. It consumes compiled verify constraints and combines verify results with twin-native structural metrics for fast iteration reports.

Deliverables:
- consume compiled verify.constraint.v1 artifacts instead of ad hoc rules JSON
- execute verify through the shared constraint contract over materialized twin state
- report twin-native structural metrics separately from verify outcomes
- keep benchmark and assess semantics out of twinning
acceptance_criteria:
1. Twinning does not define or parse a second constraint language.
2. Verify execution is sourced from the shared verify contract.
3. Structural metrics and verify outcomes remain distinguishable in the report surface.
4. Twinning does not take ownership of benchmark scoring or assess policy.
